Spokane & Inland Empire system
running to Occur d'Alene City dur
ing the coming summer.


DOUBLE TRACKING LINE.


The roadbed is already completed
past Orchard Avenue for the double
tracking Of the system through the
most thickly populated suburban
district The ties and steel, it is
declared, will be laid as soon as
the roadbed has settled sufficiently
to permit of it. Inconvenience of
travelling to and from Spokane out
of this section heretofore has been
one of the greatest drawbacks to-
ward making it more on the order
of summer and suburban home
district than of place for the rails
ing of fruit and garden truck.


Spokane, like other inland cities
of the northwest in general, owes
much of its success and growth to
the wealth produced by its tribu
tary irrigation projects, apd the
promoters of the land in Spokane
valley are laying their plans for a
permanent system of irrigation,
electric lighting and domestic wat
er supply that is determined to
make the Spokane valley another
great support to the City Beautiful,
taht shall in time to come be al
most as important to it's welfare
and to it's showing of resources as
the mines in the adjacent hills has
been in the past.


FRUIT AND TRUCK GARDEN


Fruit growing and truck garden
